# Nova Team Meeting Notes

**Date:** 2026-04-09
**Source:** AI-generated meeting notes shared by Aaron (Justin was absent — day 2 of COO retreat)
**Captured by:** GG from Aaron's share
**Related:** [[people/narek]], [[people/duncan-mountford]], [[people/chintan]], [[people/kyle]], [[people/xiaojun]], [[projects/nova]]

## Raw Content

**Disclaimer:** Generated by AI. Make sure to check for accuracy.

### Topics Covered

**1. Redis Memory and Connection Pool Issues**
Duncan, Wilson, Narek, and Alex discussed ongoing problems with Redis, including out-of-memory errors and zombie connections, and outlined recent fixes such as moving XWatch data to Postgres and implementing monitoring tools to trace and resolve these issues.
- **XWatch Data Migration:** Duncan migrated XWatch records from Redis to Postgres (OOM cause, large + long-lived records). Kept only last 20 records to keep DB clean.
- **Redis Monitoring Enhancements:** Duncan added a Redis monitor in settings page with detailed metrics to identify bottlenecks, OOM, and connection pool issues.
- **Zombie Connection Investigation:** Duncan and Narek discussed zombie connection accumulation. Duncan added verbose logging to diagnostics tool to trace origins. Narek clarified definition of a Redis connection, suggesting further investigation into worker and subscription behaviour.
- **Redis Usage Clarification:** Narek outlined Redis's intended use for run orchestration and event publishing — payloads should remain small and short-lived. Duncan confirmed current usage aligns, with Redis now primarily used for event publishing.
- **Intermittent Sandbox Issues:** Alex, Wilson, Duncan noted intermittent failures in sandbox environment, attributing to Redis connection pool issues. Agreed to prioritise fixing.

**2. Timesheet and Allocation App Development (Duncan)**
- App Structure: Weekly timesheet app for user allocations + hours; admin overview to monitor submissions; aligns with Nova single-purpose app ethos.
- Database Integration: Duncan had issues connecting to map database; Aaron helped by connecting him with Z to resolve firewall access.
- **Competitive Demo Plans: Duncan intends to demo app's rapid build capabilities to Keri, aiming to outperform Power Apps in speed and usability.**

**3. Campaign System and Vendor Management Updates**
- **Campaign System UX Improvements:** Aaron shared that Justin requested focus on enhancing existing campaign system's UX. Duncan offered support for new concepts and nuances from recent updates.
- **Vendor Onboarding Quest Progress:** Chintan prioritising vendor management quest, aiming for production-ready for Eric presentation; adding missing quests based on previous demo feedback.
- **Conditional Branching and Parallel Execution:** Chintan discussed implementing conditional branching and enabling parallel quest execution, seeking input from Aaron and Duncan.
- **DevOps Campaign Consolidation:** Saksham suggested combining DevOps-related campaigns per Tristan's advice.

**4. Outlook Interaction and Research Bias Profile Extensions (Kyle)**
- Microsoft Graph Limitations: Kyle identified limits re: delegate access visibility; proposing skill file updates to prevent unsupported actions.
- Chat-Based Outlook Interactions: Kyle observed integrating chat with Outlook/calendar reduces learning curve; plans to extend features.

**5. Quest Builder Stress Testing and Memory Node Exploration (Narek)**
- **Quest Builder Stress Testing:** Narek conducting benchmarks and stress tests on quest builder stage; results expected over next two days.
- **Mem Palace Technique Exploration:** Narek examined Mem Palace approach for memory node storage, comparing to Chroma DB's verbatim text storage + temporal graph; invited interested team members.

**6. Power Apps and Copilot Evaluation**
- Power Apps Usability Concerns: Wilson and Duncan expressed frustration with complexity and learning curve.
- Copilot Feature Investigation: Alex suggested Wilson/Duncan consult Tristan (has premium Copilot access).

**7. Quest Migration and Import Tool Development (Xiaojun + Duncan)**
- Script to migrate quests/flows databases to sandbox; plans to turn into one-button import tool for Nova.

**8. AI in the Box Concept and Local Development**
- **Distributed Local Development:** Narek advocated distributing local dev capacity to team members (e.g. Mac Minis) to minimise server issues. Wilson agreed.
- **Cloud Database Sharing:** Aaron proposed sharing cloud database while building quests locally — only DB needs to remain cloud-based. Narek supported.

**9. Client Intake and Package Generation Focus (Xiaojun + Wilson)**
- Intake class for retrieving client info + generating packages; production readiness goal.

**10. Power BI and Interaction Development (Alex)**
- Power BI debugging; plans to develop interactions for thematic exposure analysis with Manar, Mario, Lawrence.

### Follow-up Tasks Assigned
- **Duncan:** More verbose logging to Redis diagnostics tool to trace zombie connection sources
- **Duncan + Narek:** Investigate and tidy up worker-to-Redis connection handling, especially builder chat workers

## Context
Team meeting held the day after the failed April 8 COO retreat. Justin absent (day 2 of COO retreat). Team discussing recovery work, primarily Redis stability and Campaign system preparation for the next (April 17) decision deadline.

This is the first full team meeting since the retreat, and the first real look at how the team is distributing work without Justin in the room.

## GG's Interpretation (at time of capture)

### Technical layer

**Narek's Redis diagnosis is correct and senior.**
He identified the root cause: Redis dedicates a separate connection per pub/sub subscription. Current pattern (ad-hoc subscriptions per WebSocket/listener) will exhaust the pool under any real load. His proposed fix — shared pub/sub connection per worker + event bus abstraction with predicate/callback pattern — is a legitimate architectural solution. This is the work of someone thinking at the infrastructure level.

**Duncan is patching, not fixing.**
Zombie reaper every 2 minutes, XWatch → Postgres migration, maxlen enforcement on data plane. These make dashboards look green but don't address root cause. Duncan himself admitted he's still "digging deeper" — meaning the patches are containment, not resolution.

**Duncan is spreading thin: 4 parallel fronts**
1. Redis zombie reaper + monitoring
2. Map allocation node integration
3. Timesheet app for Keri demo (new!)
4. Data normalization node (background work)

Justin's public post asked Duncan to focus on Quest Builder stability with Narek. Duncan is instead splitting attention across 4 things. This is a focus failure.

**No commitment timeline on Narek's fix.**
Duncan wrote: "I'll be interested to see what the locust test returns once we have your proposed fix in place." No commitment on *who does it, when, or under what priority*. A critical-path architectural fix is sitting in "let's see" status while zombie reapers paper over the symptoms.

**"AI in the box" / local dev discussion = implicit infra admission.**
When senior engineers start proposing "let's run on local Mac Minis," they're saying the cloud infra can't scale. This is the team quietly admitting what they can't say out loud. Aaron's cloud-DB-sharing proposal was a practical hedge.

**Narek's stress test results coming in 2 days** means baseline perf is *unknown* until then. Which means Justin's assertion that "Campaign Builder actually works reliably" is unsupported by actual measurement.

### Political layer

**Duncan building his own direct line to Keri via timesheet app demo.** This is new and significant. Aaron's primary distinctive asset at Mawer is the direct relationship with Keri through check-in. Duncan is attempting to establish a parallel channel. Not hostile — just survival instinct. But Aaron should notice. If Duncan succeeds, Keri has two "vibe-deep" engineers demoing things to her, diluting Aaron's unique position.

**Chintan will pull Aaron into scope creep.**
"Chintan discussed efforts to implement conditional branching... seeking input from Aaron and Duncan" — this is the explicit scope creep signal. Conditional branching and parallel execution are *platform features*, not UX polish. Aaron must deflect. Suggested framing (prepared in meeting prep): "These are Quest Builder capabilities; let's coordinate with Duncan/Narek on stable primitives first; I'll focus on orchestrator/manager UX flow."

**Alex is on Power BI debugging** — this is his actual work. His Nova "involvement" remains narrative-level (suggestions, presentations, inspirational shares), not engineering commitment. Confirms the Political Operator pattern.

**No one mentioned Justin's post.** Interesting omission. Team is heads-down on execution without rallying around the narrative. Could be a subtle signal that the team's internal morale doesn't match Justin's public optimism.

### Aaron's position in the meeting

**Clean and well-executed:**
- Helped Duncan with firewall (Z connection) — useful, not owning
- Shared Justin's UX polish assignment transparently
- Proposed cloud DB sharing (technical contribution, not commitment)
- Did not commit to conditional branching / parallel execution despite Chintan's opening

This is the Trusted Operator pattern working as designed. Helpful, focused, boundary-aware.

### Signals for the week ahead

1. **Narek's stress test results in 2 days** will reveal whether the platform can survive any realistic load.
2. **Chintan's vendor onboarding demo to Eric** is another parallel deadline adding pressure.
3. **Duncan's Keri demo of timesheet app** will happen — watch if/when it gets scheduled.
4. **Conditional branching pressure on Aaron** will likely increase; hold the line.
5. **Justin's in-person meeting with Aaron on April 10** will set the tone for the sprint.
